title: Museum open day and pop-up market draw strong community support
description: GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - A successful open day...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - A successful open day and pop-up thrift market at the historical Reinet House in Graaff-Reinet drew strong community support on Sunday morning, 14 December.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - A successful open day and pop-up thrift market at the historical Reinet House in Graaff-Reinet drew strong community support on Sunday morning, 14 December."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The Graaff-Reinet Museum’s annual open day, combined with its first-ever pop-up market, proved to be a resounding success, attracting approximately 150 visitors."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Four of the museum’s five sites were open to the public, creating a lively yet relaxed atmosphere.","html":"Four of the museum’s five sites were open to the public, creating a lively yet relaxed atmosphere."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The event enjoyed particularly strong attendance from members of the community, who visited the museum after attending church services, contributing to a steady flow of visitors throughout the morning.","html":"The event enjoyed particularly strong attendance from members of the community, who visited the museum after attending church services, contributing to a steady flow of visitors throughout the morning."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The positive energy was evident across the museum complex, with families, friends and visitors from outside the town taking time to explore the exhibitions and buildings.","html":"The positive energy was evident across the museum complex, with families, friends and visitors from outside the town taking time to explore the exhibitions and buildings."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Among the visitors were Dr Randal Albertus and his family, who travelled from Sasolburg to attend the open day,” said Dr Anziske Kayster, a prominent cultural historian, heritage practitioner, and the manager of the Graaff-Reinet museum.","html":"“Among the visitors were Dr Randal Albertus and his family, who travelled from Sasolburg to attend the open day,” said Dr Anziske Kayster, a prominent cultural historian, heritage practitioner, and the manager of the Graaff-Reinet museum."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Dr Albertus, an environmental scientist with a particular interest in fossils, praised the experience, describing the morning as ‘fantastic’, marked by remarkable history, a peaceful atmosphere and staff who were exceptionally helpful and friendly.","html":"“Dr Albertus, an environmental scientist with a particular interest in fossils, praised the experience, describing the morning as ‘fantastic’, marked by remarkable history, a peaceful atmosphere and staff who were exceptionally helpful and friendly."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The family enjoyed refreshments from the food stalls at the pop-up market, while young Gabriel showed a keen interest in the Porcupine Quill House located in the basement of Reinet House.”","html":"The family enjoyed refreshments from the food stalls at the pop-up market, while young Gabriel showed a keen interest in the Porcupine Quill House located in the basement of Reinet House.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Kayster said the market was well supported by both visitors and local vendors, encouraging the museum to consider making it a regular feature on its events calendar.","html":"Kayster said the market was well supported by both visitors and local vendors, encouraging the museum to consider making it a regular feature on its events calendar."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The Graaff-Reinet Museum extends its sincere thanks to all who attended and supported the open day and pop-up market, and invites the public to save the date for next year’s event and once again join in celebrating our town’s rich heritage,” Kayster said.","html":"“The Graaff-Reinet Museum extends its sincere thanks to all who attended and supported the open day and pop-up market, and invites the public to save the date for next year’s event and once again join in celebrating our town’s rich heritage,” Kayster said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Museum open day and pop-up market draw strong community support
